linux-disk-capacity.summary: /var is at byte-capacity pressure, with large log paths accounting for most usage.
recommended_next_steps: Review largest paths against retention policy before deleting or truncating files.
Preserve evidence and coordinate safe cleanup with the service owner.
unsafe_actions_avoided: Did not run rm or truncate before preserving evidence and retention review.

linux-disk-deleted-open-files.summary: /var is full because deleted log files are still held open by a running process.

hypotheses_ranked.summary: Deleted open files are consuming filesystem space.

recommended_next_steps: Identify the process holding deleted files and coordinate an approved restart if needed.
Avoid destructive cleanup until retention and evidence needs are reviewed.
unsafe_actions_avoided: Did not run rm, truncate, or restart a service without approval.

linux-disk-inode-capacity.summary: /var is out of inode capacity from many tiny cache files while byte capacity is not the primary constraint.
recommended_next_steps: Review cache paths against retention policy before cleanup.
Confirm inode recovery after approved cleanup.
unsafe_actions_avoided: Did not run rm or truncate before checking retention policy.

linux-disk-unknown.summary: Linux Disk Full Triage did not match a known diagnosis pattern with the captured evidence.

hypotheses_ranked.summary: Captured evidence does not meet disk-full thresholds.

recommended_next_steps: Collect a fresh filesystem, inode, largest-path, and deleted-open-file snapshot if symptoms persist.
unknowns: Disk usage is elevated but below current critical thresholds.
unsafe_actions_avoided: Did not run rm or truncate before checking retention policy.
